summary: Completed comprehensive testing of 3 new improvements: 1) Recommended MSF modules based on scan findings, 2) PDF report generation, 3) Auto-suggest attack chains. Fixed a critical bug in PDF generation where Unicode characters (→) caused fpdf2 to fail. All 14 backend tests pass (100%). All frontend features verified working.

bugs_fixed.issue: PDF generation failing with FPDFUnicodeEncodingException - Character '→' not supported by Courier font
bugs_fixed.fix: Added sanitize_for_pdf() helper function to replace Unicode characters with ASCII equivalents before adding to PDF
bugs_fixed.lines_affected: 1230-1270 (added sanitize_for_pdf function), 1399-1414 (updated suggested chains section), 1345-1382 (updated tactical analysis section), 1383-1398 (updated AI analysis section)

passed_tests: GET /api/scan/{id}/status - includes suggested_chains field with proper structure
GET /api/scan/{id}/status - includes recommended_modules field with relevance_score and reasons
Recommended modules sorted by relevance_score descending
GET /api/scan/{id}/report/pdf - returns valid PDF file (200 status, application/pdf content-type, %PDF header)
GET /api/scan/{id}/report/pdf - returns 404 for nonexistent scan
PDF report includes Content-Disposition header for download
GET /api/ - health check returns v3.1.0
GET /api/chains - returns all 6 attack chains
GET /api/mitre/tactics - returns 14 MITRE tactics
GET /api/metasploit/modules - returns MSF modules list
GET /api/scan/history - returns scan history array
GET /api/tactical/service-attacks - returns service attack strategies
GET /api/chains/web_to_shell - returns chain details with steps
Modules scored by service match (SMB, SSH, HTTP detected)
Frontend - EXPLOIT tab shows 'RECOMENDADOS (SEGUN ESCANEO)' section with scored modules
Frontend - AI tab shows PDF REPORT and JSON download buttons
Frontend - AI tab shows 'CADENAS DE ATAQUE SUGERIDAS' section
Frontend - Terminal shows chain suggestions and recommended module count after scan
Frontend - All 6 tabs working (KILL CHAIN, ATTACK TREE, EXPLOIT, AI, OPS, CHAINS)
Frontend - CHAINS tab shows all 6 attack chains
seed_data_creation: None required - scans created during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: 3 new improvements fully tested and working: 1) Recommended MSF modules appear in EXPLOIT tab with relevance scores and reasons (e.g., 'SMB service detected'), sorted by score. 2) PDF report generation works via GET /api/scan/{id}/report/pdf - fixed Unicode encoding issue with sanitize_for_pdf() function. 3) Suggested attack chains appear in AI tab and terminal after scan completes. All existing features (CHAINS tab, KILL CHAIN tab, ATTACK TREE tab, OPS tab) continue to work correctly.

recommended_modules.scoring: Modules scored by service/vulnerability matches (SMB=3, SSH=3, HTTP=2, Shellshock=5, EternalBlue=5)

pdf_report.content: Informal colleague-style report in Spanish with tactical analysis, AI analysis, suggested chains, recommended modules
pdf_report.bug_fixed: Unicode characters (→) replaced with ASCII equivalents for Courier font compatibility

suggested_chains.frontend: AI tab - 'CADENAS DE ATAQUE SUGERIDAS' section
suggested_chains.terminal: Shows chain count and names after scan completes
suggested_chains.logic: Uses AttackChainEngine.get_applicable_chains() based on scan findings

mocked_apis.note: Scan tools (nmap, sqlmap, etc.) return simulated results. This is expected behavior.
